steps.title: Separate the two sentences
steps.content: The first complete idea is that Laila Nehme traveled to Hegra to study its ancient tombs. The next sentence begins with a participial phrase, "Built into the rocky outcrops of a vast desert," and then has the independent clause "these burial chambers seem to blend seamlessly with nature."

steps.title: Reject fused structures
steps.content: B creates a comma splice by joining the first independent clause to the next sentence with only a comma. C incorrectly coordinates "study" and "built" as if they were parallel actions. D fuses the tombs description into one confusing sentence with no clear boundary. The answer is A.
